title: Smoking status cardiac review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports current and former tobacco status, product use, willingness and readiness to quit, previous attempts, motivation, confidence, barriers, and dependence assessment including smoking rate and time to first cigarette.

source.title: Practical Guideline for Management of Tobacco Dependence
source.population: People who use tobacco, recently stopped tobacco, do not currently use tobacco, or may be exposed to second-hand smoke.
source.setting: Clinical and public-health tobacco-use identification, assessment, cessation support, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Direct UAE source for documenting tobacco status, exposure, quit readiness, prior attempts, barriers, clinician-discussed support, referral, and follow-up; medicine names, doses, and autonomous cessation instructions are not mapped.

assessment.text: Supports current and former tobacco status, product use, willingness and readiness to quit, previous attempts, motivation, confidence, barriers, and dependence assessment including smoking rate and time to first cigarette.
assessment.rationale: The UAE MOHAP guideline directly supports documenting current or recent tobacco use, product and quantity context, dependence-related questions, prior attempts, and readiness when discussed; it does not assume smoking or generate cessation advice.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports current and former tobacco status, product use, willingness and readiness to quit, previous attempts, motivation, confidence, barriers, and dependence assessment including smoking rate and time to first cigarette.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular risk review and documented lifestyle discussion without autonomously generating advice.
assessment.rationale: NG238 supports recording smoking as one clinician-assessed cardiovascular-risk factor, patient preferences, and an entered plan without calculating risk or prescribing an intervention.

source.title: Cardiovascular disease: risk assessment and reduction, including lipid modification
source.population: Adults receiving cardiovascular risk assessment or lipid-modification review for primary or secondary prevention.
source.setting: Primary care and other clinician-led cardiovascular prevention services.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult cardiovascular risk and lipid-modification assessment and monitoring; international only and not directly validated for UAE pathways.
